question: Explain the relationship between yield curve inversions and recessions. Are there recent academic papers on this topic?
answer: Yield curve inversions have been observed to precede many of the recessions in the United States, with the inversion of the yield curve being seen as a potential predictor of economic downturns [Wikipedia: Yield curve inversion]. Recent academic papers have further explored this relationship, using various methodologies to assess the predictive power of yield curve inversions [arXiv: papers on yield curve inversion and recession]. Historical data supports the notion that yield curve inversions are often followed by recessions, although the lag between inversion and recession can vary [FRED: Series related to yield curve and recession]. While yield curve inversions are not infallible predictors of recessions, they are considered an important indicator by economists and financial analysts. The ongoing research in this area aims to enhance our ability to predict recessions by better understanding the mechanisms behind yield curve inversions and their implications for the broader economy.
